edges.description: Persistent perfusion failure injures gray matter neurons and spinal tracts.
edges.target: Anterior horn and spinal tract ischemic injury

nodes.description: Hypoxic-ischemic injury often involves anterior spinal artery territory structures, damaging motor neurons and long tracts that mediate acute motor, sensory, and autonomic deficits.
nodes.id: Anterior horn and spinal tract ischemic injury

evidence.reference_title: Incomplete Anterior Spinal Artery Syndrome Responsive to Intrathecal Baclofen.
evidence.snippet: Anterior cord syndrome (ACS) occurs as a result of ischemia in the territory of the anterior spinal artery (ASA).

evidence.reference_title: Incomplete Anterior Spinal Artery Syndrome Responsive to Intrathecal Baclofen.
evidence.snippet: the underlying neural structures responsible for these symptoms include the corticospinal tracts and anterior horns, anterolateral spinothalamic tracts, and lateral horns, respectively.

nodes.description: Aortic pathology, vertebral or aortic dissection, arteriosclerosis, embolism, systemic hypotension, perioperative interruption of collateral supply, or rarer embolic mechanisms can reduce spinal cord perfusion and initiate hypoxic-ischemic injury.

evidence.reference_title: Spinal cord ischemia: aetiology, clinical syndromes and imaging features.
evidence.snippet: Aetiologies of infarcts were arteriosclerosis of the aorta and vertebral arteries (23.6%), aortic surgery or interventional aneurysm repair (11%) and aortic and vertebral artery dissection (11%), and in 23.6%, aetiology remained unclear.

evidence.reference_title: Spinal Cord Infarction: Clinical and Neuroradiological Clues of a Rare Stroke Subtype
evidence.snippet: Several etiologies can be considered, considering traditional vascular risk factors and diseases affecting the aorta and its main branches
